# @021.is/spine-cache — LLM usage

> TTL + tag-invalidation cache with single-flight dedupe: N concurrent callers for the same key share one backend call.

Install: bun add @021.is/spine-cache

## Imports
- `import { makeMemoryCache, makeKey } from "@021.is/spine-cache"` — single entry

## API
- `makeMemoryCache()` — returns a `MemoryCache`:
  - `fetch<T>(key, fn, opts?)` — cache-aside; concurrent calls for the same key dedupe to one `fn` run. `CacheOptions`: `ttlMs?` (default 60_000), `tags?`
  - `invalidate(key)` — drop one entry
  - `invalidateTag(tag)` — drop every entry tagged `tag`
  - `clear()` / `size()` — for tests + observability
- `makeKey(prefix, ...parts)` — build a readable key (`user:u_1:lang:en`); nullish parts dropped
- Types: `MemoryCache`, `CacheOptions`

## Example
```ts
import { makeMemoryCache, makeKey } from "@021.is/spine-cache";

const cache = makeMemoryCache();

const user = await cache.fetch(
  makeKey("user", id),
  () => db.user.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id } }),
  { ttlMs: 60_000, tags: ["user", `user:${id}`] },
);

cache.invalidateTag("user"); // wipes ALL user entries
```

## Notes
- Zero deps, process-local (no Redis). `tags` are `revalidateTag`-compatible naming.
- Default `ttlMs` is 60_000 when omitted.
